brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is the Lanczos/Krylov three-term recurrence built from the symplectic (Poisson-bracket) structure of phase space to track complexity growth in chaotic dynamics, whereas Paper B's machinery is a stochastic urn sampling model whose per-round survival and coverage rates generate containment and discovery curves for evolving URL populations; these are unrelated governing processes with no role-for-role correspondence. The only overlap is the coincidental word 'shell' (an energy shell / Krylov-chain depth in A versus a core-shell urn decomposition in B), which is shared vocabulary rather than shared mechanism.
